headlines.text: SpaceX goes public on Nasdaq under SPCX, soaring ~27% above its record $75B IPO price
Trump claims Iran deal is 'approved' and cancels new strikes; Tehran says nothing is finalized
Olivia Rodrigo drops third album 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' today
FISA Section 702 surveillance authority set to expire after House Democrats block short-term renewal
Knicks overcome historic 29-point deficit to take 3-1 NBA Finals lead over Spurs
UK Defense Secretary John Healey quits, warns Britain's military lacks resources for rising threats
Vance Boelter pleads guilty in politically motivated assassination of Minnesota Rep. Melissa Hortman
WTI crude oil falls below $85, hitting 8-week low on hopes of imminent US-Iran peace deal
Supreme Court blocks Alabama from using nitrogen gas in execution, delaying Jeffery Lee's death sentence
Pope Leo XIV visits Spain's Canary Islands migrant hotspot, throws wreath into sea in tribute to those who drowned

breaking.text: BREAKING: Trump cancels Iran strikes, claims deal 'approved' — Tehran disputes finalized agreement as US-Iran peace talks reach critical point ahead of G7 summit

stories.hed: Trump Says Deal Done, Iran Says 'Not So Fast,' Oil Says 'Bye'
stories.dek: Trump called off new strikes and declared a 'great settlement' with Iran — Tehran's response was essentially a shrug emoji in diplomat form. A draft deal reportedly includes Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days, which oil traders treated as gospel, sending WTI below $85 for the first time in 8 weeks.

stories.hed: UK Defense Secretary Quits, Says Military Is Dangerously Underfunded
stories.dek: British Defense Secretary John Healey resigned, accusing PM Keir Starmer's government of failing to adequately fund the military amid rising threats from Russia and increasing NATO demands. The resignation rattles the UK's defense posture at a moment when Europe is already on edge.

stories.hed: FISA Spy Tool Set to Go Dark After Democrats Block Renewal
stories.dek: House Democrats blocked a short-term extension of FISA Section 702 — the surveillance program credited with stopping a Taylor Swift terror plot — citing concerns about Trump's pick of Bill Pulte as acting DNI. The House then left town until June 23, as one does during a national security crisis.

stories.hed: SpaceX IPO Is the Biggest in History and Wall Street Is Losing Its Mind
stories.dek: SpaceX debuted on Nasdaq today under SPCX at $150/share — 11% above its $135 IPO price — and climbed as high as $176, raising $75 billion in the largest public offering ever recorded. Retail investors who requested 1,000 shares through Robinhood received an average of 17, which is somehow both fair and insulting.

stories.hed: PPI Surged 6.5% Year-Over-Year in May — The Iran War Is Now Officially an Inflation Problem
stories.dek: Producer prices just posted their highest 12-month gain since November 2022, and the culprit is exactly what you think: a war-driven energy shock squeezing supply chains from the Strait of Hormuz to your local gas station. The Fed meets next week, and traders are already pricing in a rate hike by year-end — the economy's version of 'things are fine.'

markets.context: Markets gyrated as Iran peace deal optimism crushed oil prices while the blockbuster SpaceX IPO sucked liquidity from tech, leaving the Dow up and Nasdaq in the red.

gas.note: The Iran war turned a family road trip into a financial decision — and not a fun one.

popCulture.hed: Olivia Rodrigo Releases Third Album 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' Today — And TikTok Is Already Unraveling
popCulture.dek: The three-time Grammy winner dropped her most experimental record yet today via Geffen Records, featuring a guest appearance from The Cure's Robert Smith — her first-ever collaboration. Critics are calling it her most mature and musically adventurous album, which means Gen Z is already sobbing in their cars.

genZ.definition: A TikTok format where creators show a situation that starts innocently but spirals into chaos, timed to a blasting sax solo that suddenly explodes at full volume mid-video — it's become the universal sound for 'things are about to go very wrong.' The trend is dominating June FYPs because it perfectly captures the national mood of watching peace deal negotiations unfold in real time.
genZ.usage: Trump: 'We made a great settlement with Iran.' Iran: 'We did not.' *saxophone gets louder*

tldr: SpaceX became the biggest IPO in history today while Trump and Iran argued over whether they made a deal — oil fell 3% just from the vibes. Olivia Rodrigo also dropped her third album, ensuring that every American's Friday evening will be either on the trading floor or crying to sad pop songs.
